rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Solid pulmonary nodules in the left lower lobe, measuring up to 3 mm in diameter. Discoid atelectasis in the left upper lobe and right lower lobe. Lungs are unremarkable. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial effusion measuring up to 5 mm in thickness. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy. In the mid portion of the right kidney, a round hypodense lesion measures up to 6 mm in diameter, with density of -34 HU.
rad_impression_en:
1. Pericardial effusion measuring up to 5 mm.

2. Solid pulmonary nodules in the left lower lobe measuring up to 3 mm. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, no further CT follow-up is required.

3. Discoid atelectasis in both lungs.

4. Hypodense lesion in the right kidney (likely a benign cyst; no further follow-up is required).

5. Cholecystectomy.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Discoid atelectasis in the left upper lobe and right lower lobe, paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced pulmonary fibrosis in the right lower lobe, clinically insignificant. In the upper lobe of the left lung there is a subpleural ground-glass area, may be post-inflammatory or interstitial changes.
In the left lower lobe:
- Subpleural solid nodule measures 4 mm (series 3, image 160).
- Subpleural solid nodule measures 4 mm (series 3, image 144).
In the right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 4 mm (series 3, image 109).
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. 6 mm osteosclerotic lesion of T12 vertebral body. 

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy. In the mid portion of the right kidney, a round hypodense lesion measures up to 6 mm in diameter, with density of -34 HU.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid pulmonary nodules in both lungs measure up to 4 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.
In the upper lobe of the left lung there is a subpleural ground-glass area, may be post-inflammatory or interstitial changes.

2. Fat containing lesion in the right kidney. ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273) suggest the following: Benign uncomplicated angiomyolipoma. No need for further work-up.


DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Solid micronodules up to 2 mm in both lungs (no interval change), calcified granulomas up to 5 mm (no interval change), and discoid atelectasis. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Calcifications in right hilar lymph nodes. Hiatal hernia with herniation of part of the stomach into the lower posterior mediastinum, measuring 5.9 × 6.3 cm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as severe. Calcification of the aortic and mitral valves.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with severe wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Retrolisthesis of L1 and L2 by 2 mm (Meyerding grade 1). Bone demineralization. Healing fractures of the left 5th and 6th ribs.

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy. In the upper pole of the right kidney, a lesion with density 0 HU, measuring about 1.0 cm, with indistinct margins (no change in size compared with CT chest dated March 06, 2025). In the upper pole of the left kidney, a lesion with density 44 HU, measuring about 1.6 × 1.2 cm, with indistinct margins (no definite change in size compared with CT chest dated March 06, 2025 and October 25, 2025).
rad_impression_en:
1. Multiple stable micronodules in both lungs. Fleischner Society recommendations may not be applicable considering the clinical history. Follow-up per oncology follow-up protocol. Discoid atelectasis in both lungs. Calcified granulomas in both lungs and calcifications in intrathoracic lymph nodes are consistent with prior infection.

2. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Calcification of the aortic and mitral valves.

3. Severe atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Hiatal hernia.

5. Stable right renal lesion; per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018, no further evaluation is required. Stable left renal lesion; per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018, no further evaluation is required.

6. Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Retrolisthesis of L1 and L2 (Meyerding grade 1). Bone demineralization. Healing fractures of the left 5th and 6th ribs.

7. Cholecystectomy.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the lung bases, bilateral gravitational changes, discoid atelectasis, and interstitial changes. Multiple calcified granulomas up to 5 mm (no interval change). A few solid micronodules up to 2 mm in both lungs (no interval change). No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Calcifications in hilar lymph nodes. Hiatal hernia with herniation of part of the stomach into the lower posterior mediastinum, measuring 5.9 × 6.3 cm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as severe. Calcification of the aortic and mitral valves.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with severe wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Retrolisthesis of L1 and L2 by 3 mm (Meyerding grade 1), pronounced erosive changes in the endplates of L2 and L3, unchanged. Bone demineralization, with decreased vertebral body density to 50 HU. Healing fractures of the left 5th and 6th ribs.

Upper abdomen: Please see separately dictated abdomen CT, performed concurrently.
val_impression_en:
1. A few stable micronodules in both lungs. Fleischner Society recommendations may not be applicable considering the clinical history. Follow-up per oncology follow-up protocol.

2. Calcified granulomas in both lungs and calcifications in intrathoracic lymph nodes are consistent with prior infection and require no follow-up.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

4. Severe atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Calcification of the aortic and mitral valves.

5. Hiatal hernia.

6. Retrolisthesis of L1 and L2 (Meyerding grade 1). Bone demineralization. 


DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectatic changes in the lingular segments on the left, paravertebrally in the right lower lobe.
A ground-glass nodule of moderate attenuation in the right upper lobe measures up to 1.5 × 1.2 cm (image 53).
A right perifissural calcification measures up to 3 mm.
No pleural effusion.
The trachea and large bronchi are patent; the walls are partially calcified.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass is identified.
A round station 8 lymph node is enlarged to 1.2 cm in short axis.

Heart: No cardiomegaly.
No significant pericardial effusion.
Aortic valve calcification.
High-density linear inclusions in the walls of the coronary arteries - coronary artery stenting of the right coronary artery, left anterior descending artery, and probably circumflex artery - assessment of coronary calcification is limited in this setting.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; wall calcification is present.
Aberrant right subclavian artery (arteria lusoria).
The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation.
Decreased density of vertebral bodies at T11-L1 to +140 HU.

Upper abdomen: Calcification of the abdominal aortic wall.
High-density inclusions are seen in the stomach (about +900 HU/+1100 HU).
rad_impression_en:
1. CT appearance of a ground-glass nodule in the right upper lobe: per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, follow-up CT is recommended in 6-12 months, then every 2 years until 5 years.

2. Intrathoracic lymphadenopathy of a station 8 lymph node.

3. Coronary artery stenting.

4. Aortic valve calcification.

5. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic and abdominal aorta. Aberrant right subclavian artery (arteria lusoria).

6. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased vertebral body density at T11-L1.

7. High-density content in the lumen of the stomach.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectatic changes in the lingular segments on the left, in the lung bases. In the right lower lobe there is a paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced fibrosis.
In the lung parenchyma, solid nodules are identified, in series 5, presented as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- A ground-glass nodule measures up to 1.5 × 1.2 cm (image 53).
Right lower lobe:
- A subpleural ground-glass nodule measures up to 5 mm (image 175).
- A part part-solid nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 166).
Left lower lobe:
- A part part-solid nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 159).
A right perifissural calcified nodule measures up to 3 mm, requires no follow-up.
No pleural effusion. The trachea and large bronchi are patent; the walls are partially calcified. Wall thickening and secretion of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi. 

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass is identified. A round para-esophageal node (station 8) is enlarged to 1.2 cm in short axis (image 175).

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as severe.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; wall calcification is present. Aberrant right subclavian artery (arteria lusoria). The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased density of vertebral bodies at T11-L1 to +140 HU (CT signs of demineralization).

Upper abdomen: Calcification of the abdominal aortic wall.
val_impression_en:
1. A ground-glass nodule measures 1.5 × 1.2 cm in the right upper lobe. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: Initial chest CT at 6-12 months to confirm persistence. If stable, chest CT every 2 years until 5 years. Part Part-solid and ground-glass nodules in both lungs measure up to 5 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations suggest the following: Follow-up chest CT at 3-6 months to confirm persistence. If stable, consider chest CT at 2 and 4 years. 
Wall thickening and secretion of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi may represent bronchitis.

2. Intrathoracic lymphadenopathy of a para-esophageal lymph node.

3. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs.

4. Aortic valve calcification. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic and abdominal aorta. Aberrant right subclavian artery (arteria lusoria).





DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectatic changes in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and basal segments on the left. No nodules requiring follow-up are identified.
No pleural effusion.
Trachea and central bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly.
No coronary calcification.
Pericardial effusion measures up to 1.1 cm in thickness.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification.
Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Transpedicular fixation at T12-L2. Decreased vertebral body height at T5-T6 up to 30%. Osteosclerotic lesion in the head of the left humerus measuring up to 5 mm. Healing fracture of the posterior left 10th rib.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.

Thyroid gland: In the right thyroid lobe, there is a hypodense lesion with well-defined margins and fluid density, measuring up to 7 × 6 mm.
rad_impression_en:
1. No pulmonary nodules requiring follow-up.

2. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

3. Small pericardial effusion.

4. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased vertebral body height at T5-T6. Osteosclerotic lesion in the head of the left humerus (Bone-RADS 1), likely enostosis. Transpedicular fixation at T12-L2. Healing fracture of the left 10th rib.

5. Thyroid nodular changes: per ACR White Paper recommendations (Hoang, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2015;12:143-150), no further follow-up is required.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectatic changes in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and basal segments on the left. No nodules requiring follow-up are identified.
No pleural effusion.
Trachea and central bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly.
No coronary calcification.
Pericardial effusion measures up to 1.1 cm in thickness.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification.
Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Transpedicular fixation at T12-L2. Bone island in the head of the left humerus measuring up to 5 mm. Healed fracture of the left 10th rib.

Upper abdomen: Hepatic steatosis (liver parenchyma density up to 30 HU).

Thyroid gland: In the right thyroid lobe, there is a hypodense lesion, measuring up to 4 mm. Per ACR White Paper recommendations (Hoang, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2015;12:143-150), no further follow-up is required.
val_impression_en:
1. No pulmonary nodules requiring follow-up.

2. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

3. Small pericardial effusion.

4. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased vertebral body height at T5-T6. Osteosclerotic lesion in the head of the left humerus (Bone-RADS 1), likely enostosis. Transpedicular fixation at T12-L2. Healing fracture of the left 10th rib.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Respiratory motion artifacts due to inadequate breath-hold during scanning. Gravitational changes predominantly in lung bases.
Pulmonary nodules in series 4 are as follows:
Left upper lobe:
- Calcified nodule measuring up to 4 mm, slice 61.
- Micronodule measuring up to 2 mm, slice 73.
- Solid nodule measuring 4 mm, slice 111.
- Solid nodule measuring 3 mm, slice 166.
Right upper lobe:
- Micronodule measuring up to 2 mm, slice 67.
- Calcified nodule measuring up to 3 mm, slice 119.
- Solid nodule measuring 3 mm, slice 182.
- Solid nodule measuring 3 mm, slice 186.
- Polygonal subpleural solid nodule measuring 3 mm, slice 180.
- Centrilobular tree-in-bud nodules, slices 192-914.
Right lower lobe:
- Calcified micronodules measuring up to 2 mm, slices 151 and 178.
- Solid nodule measuring up to 3 mm, slice 219.
A few areas of paraseptal emphysema.
Minimal left apical fibrosis.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: Heart is enlarged - cardiothoracic index >58.
No clinically significant pericardial effusion.
Coronary calcification is present in the left main and left anterior descending arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild.
Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta: Dilatation up to 5 mm.
Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.7 cm, right pulmonary artery up to 3.0 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, left-sided scoliotic deformity.
Postoperative changes in the humeral heads. Osteoarthritis of the shoulder joints.

Upper abdomen: Gallbladder stone measuring up to 2.0 cm in diameter.
rad_impression_en:
1. Multiple solid nodules, including calcified nodules and micronodules. According to Fleischner Society recommendations, no follow-up is required in low-risk patients; in high-risk patients, optional CT in 12 months. A single small nodule characteristic of an intrathoracic lymph node requires no follow-up.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.7 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension. Right pulmonary artery measures 3.0 cm. Cardiomegaly.

3. Dilatation of the ascending aorta.

4. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as mild.
The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.
Aortic valve calcification.

5. Gallbladder stone.

6. Minimal left apical fibrosis.

7. A few areas of paraseptal emphysema (mild emphysema).
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: The membranous portion of the trachea prolapses into the lumen (study performed on incomplete inspiration). Respiratory motion artifacts. Gravitational changes predominantly in lung bases. Minimal bilateral apical scarring.
Pulmonary nodules in series 4 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Micronodule measures up to 2 mm, image 67.
- Calcified nodule measures up to 3 mm, image 119, healed granuloma, requires no follow-up.
Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 3 mm, image 182.
- Solid nodule measures 3 mm, image 186.
- Polygonal subpleural solid nodule measures 3 mm, image 180.
- A few centrilobular micronodules, image 192-199.
Right lower lobe:
- Calcified micronodules measure up to 2 mm, images 151 and 178, healed granuloma, requires no follow-up.
- Solid nodule measures up to 4 mm, image 219.
Left upper lobe:
- Calcified nodule measures up to 4 mm, image 61, healed granuloma, requires no follow-up.
- Solid nodule measures 4 mm, image 111.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: Heart is enlarged, cardiothoracic index 0.59. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries (image 222), qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: The ascending aorta is dilated to 4.7 cm. Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.7 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Screws in the humeral heads, degenerative changes of the shoulder joints.

Upper abdomen: Gallbladder stone measures up to 2.0 cm.
val_impression_en:
1. Multiple solid nodules measure up to 4 mm in both lungs. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.7 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

3. Dilatation of the ascending aorta up to 4.7 cm.

4. Incidental coronary artery calcifications are present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Cardiomegaly.
